comments.text: Porubský (Š. Porubský, On MacMahon's segmented numbers and related sequences, Nieuw Arch. Wisk., (3) 25 (1977) 403-408) showed that $$\liminf \frac{a_k \log \log k}{k \log^{1 + \epsilon} k} = 0$$holds for all $\epsilon > 0$ and every strictly increasing sequence of $a_k$ such that every positive integer can be written as a sum of one or more consecutive elements. In particular this holds for the sequence at hand. I don't have access to the Porubský paper, but I found the above claim in Guy, R. K. Research Problems become Unsolved Problems. The American Mathematical Monthly, 88(10) (1981), 755–761. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
